data.title: Taysha Gene Therapies regains full rights to TSHA-102
data.description:
• Taysha Gene Therapies (NASDAQ:TSHA) has announced that it has regained complete rights to its leading program, TSHA-102, which is currently being evaluated for Rett syndrome.
  • The option agreement established in 2022 between Astellas and Taysha has now expired.
  • This agreement had previously allowed Astellas the exclusive opportunity to negotiate for a license to TSHA-102 for Rett syndrome, along with certain rights related to control transactions involving Taysha.
  • The stock is down about 10% in morning trade.

data.title: Huntington Bancshares lifts NII growth guidance after Q3 earnings beat
data.description:
Huntington Bancshares' (NASDAQ:HBAN) third-quarter earnings, released on Friday, came in better than expected, driven by strong growth in net interest income and fee revenues. The bank also upwardly revised its full-year NII guidance to reflect lower deposit pricing and earnings asset growth.

HBAN shares gained 1.5% in morning trading.

It now expects 2025 NII will climb 10%-11% (midpoint 10.5%) from the FY24 baseline of $5.398B, implying $5.94B-$5.99B. That compares with the previous target of 8%-9% (midpoint 8.5%) and the $5.99B Visible Alpha estimate.

In addition, the regional lender upped its noninterest income growth goal to ~7% from 4%-6% (midpoint 5%) in the earlier outlook. It also raised its guide for noninterest expense to ~6.5% from 5%-6% (midpoint 5.5%).

Turning to Q3 results, adjusted EPS of $0.40, beating the $0.37 average analyst estimate, rose from $0.35 in Q2 and $0.33 in last year's Q3.

Q3 revenue (fully taxable equivalent) of $2.15B, vs. $2.05B consensus, climbed from $1.95B in Q2 and $1.89B a year ago.

"We continue to deliver balanced, above-peer growth by acquiring new customers, deepening relationships, and expanding both net interest income and diversified fee revenues," said Chairman, President and CEO Steve Steinour.

Net interest income ("FTE") was $1.52B in Q3, up from $1.48B in Q2 and $1.44B in Q3 2024.

Noninterest income of $628M accelerated from $471M in Q2 and $494M in Q3 2024.

Average total deposits rose 1% Q/Q to $1.4B. Average loans and leases increased 2% sequentially to $135.9B.

data.title: Goldman sees S&P 500 cash spending hitting $4.4 trillion in 2026 on AI capex boom
data.description:
S&P 500 cash spending is expected to rise 11% in 2026 to a record $4.4 trillion, driven by a surge in capital expenditure from large technology firms and a rebound in cash M&A, according to Goldman Sachs. 

Goldman Sachs expects 17% capex growth in the next year, boosted by continued AI investment from five hyperscalers, namely Amazon (AMZN), Google (GOOGL) (GOOG), Meta (META), Microsoft (MSFT) and Oracle (ORCL). The companies also account for 27% of S&P 500 capex.

"Healthy earnings growth, tailwinds from monetary and fiscal policy, and AI-related investments will support strong cash spending," Goldman Sachs' analyst Ryan Hammond said. 

Hammond added that while there are plenty of risks, he expects policy uncertainty to reduce and CEO confidence to continue to improve in 2026. 

S&P 500 cash spending is also expected to be boosted by the full expensing of R&D, equipment capex and some structure capex under the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, as well as ongoing AI-related investments by the largest hyperscalers. 

The consensus expects that the hyperscalers will grow capex by 20% in the next year, and Goldman Sachs believes that capex growth will surprise to the upside. 

"If the hyperscalers continue to believe the returns on AI investment are compelling enough, they have substantial capacity for more investment spending. However, this spending has constrained buyback growth among the group, which has been flat year-over-year during the past four quarters," Hammond said.

Here are some tech-related ETFs: (NYSEARCA:VGT), (NYSEARCA:IYW), (NYSEARCA:FTEC), (NYSEARCA:IXN), and (NYSEARCA:RSPT).

Artificial Intelligence/Robotics ETFs: (NASDAQ:AIQ), (NASDAQ:BOTZ), (NYSEARCA:DTEC), (NYSEARCA:GINN), (NASDAQ:ROBT), (NYSEARCA:TECB), (NASDAQ:XT), (NYSEARCA:THNQ), and (NYSEARCA:CHAT).

data.title: e.l.f. Beauty brings shoppable ads to Twitch through Amazon partnership
data.description:
• In a first of its kind collaboration, e.l.f. Beauty (NYSE:ELF) customers can now buy cosmetics directly on Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) through a partnership with Twitch.  
  • Designed to keep audiences engaged, this ad format between e.l.f. (NYSE:ELF), Amazon Ads (NASDAQ:AMZN), and Twitch allows the brand to connect with “highly-engaged communities” and consumers to purchase products in real time without disrupting viewing.
  • First launched in 2020, the e.l.f.YOU! channel on Twitch gave the company an opportunity to “game up by the space by bringing beauty into the conversation,” said Patrick O’Keefe, Chief Integrated Marketing Communications Officer at e.l.f. (NYSE:ELF). With the integration of Amazon Ads, gamers can now purchase products while on the Twitch platform, seamlessly linking e.l.f. (ELF) products to the Amazon (AMZN) platform.

data.title: Oracle's ambitious targets prompt positive reaction by analysts
data.description:
Oracle's (NYSE:ORCL) analyst day revealed ambitious long-term financial targets driven by an ever-rising demand around artificial intelligence applications and infrastructure, which prompted mostly positive reactions from analysts.

"Oracle's highly anticipated analyst day revealed updated LT targets, including $225B revenue in FY30 (representing a 31% 5YR CAGR vs. our prior estimate of ~20%)," said Piper Sandler analysts Hannah Rudoff and J.R. Herrera in a Friday investor note. "Underpinning this is an impressive 75% 5YR OCI CAGR expected to drive the cloud infrastructure business to $166B in revenue scale by FY30 (raised from $144B prior)."

Piper Sandler reiterated its Overweight rating and increased its price target to $380 from $330.

Oracle co-CEO Clay Magouyrk broke down some details on gross margins regarding the selling of GPU capacity from AI data centers.

"So as an example, let's imagine that somebody shows up and they want to buy approximately 1 gigawatt of GPU accelerators, right?," Magouryk said. "And they sign a contract for 6 years, which would be 6 years at $10B a year, it's a $60B TCV."

"I've broken the costs we have into two sections," he said. "One is what I'm calling land, data center and power. That's obviously the buildings, it's the actual power generation, it's the people cost to run that portion of the business, right? That ends up being about 35% of the cost to deliver that service."

"Now if you add up compute, networking and storage, right, all the things that you put inside of that data center, that ends up being about 65% of the cost ... But the thing to understand is when I talk about this 35% margin, it doesn't just include each year after it's running," he added. "That 35% includes, right, the fact that there's a startup cost upfront. Now obviously, if you can imagine, if you were to put a demarcation line before year 1 starts, we have an expense with no compensating revenue yet. And that can be happening across many different sites, right? We're building not one of these things, we're building 10, 20 of them at a time."

Guggenheim increased its price target on Oracle to $400 from $375. T.D. Cowen also raised their target price to $400 from $375.

However, Seeking Alpha analyst Kevin Anthony D. Arroyo said there are risks tied to Oracle reaching these lofty goals. Specifically, its heavy concentration with OpenAI (OPENAI), as about two-thirds of Oracle's nearly $500B backlog belongs to the Sam Altman-led startup.

This presents a significant customer concentration risk for Oracle, especially for a startup that has yet to turn a profit.

"The deal commits OpenAI to a rent-an-infrastructure arrangement with Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, or OCI," Arroyo said in a Thursday analysis. "This is around a $60B per year expense for OpenAI, or a total of $300B on a five-year agreement, which will start in 2027. How will OpenAI pay for this deal and even sustain it?"

data.title: Meta to add new parental controls to manage teen chats with AI characters
data.description:
Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) said Friday it is introducing new parental controls and safety measures on its platforms to watch over and manage teen interactions with AI characters.

The company said the new supervision tools will help parents make informed decisions about the AI characters their teens chat with.

Parents will now be able to turn off their teen’s access to one-on-one chats with AI characters selectively or entirely. They will also get insights into the topics their teens are chatting about with AI characters and can set time limits for daily interactions, the company said.

Meta said its AI characters are designed to not engage in discussions on topics such as self-harm, suicide, romance, or disordered eating, and that teens will only be allowed to talk about subjects like education, sports, and hobbies.

The company will roll out the features on its Teen Accounts, starting with Instagram, in the U.S., UK, Canada, and Australia early next year, with other regions to follow later.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: Crypto stocks, AXP, and more
data.description:
Stock futures dipped on Friday, driven by renewed concerns over the financial health of American regional banks and persistent trade tensions between the U.S. and China.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Achieve Life Sciences (ACHV) +27%, Disc Medicine (IRON) +20%, and Revolution Medicines (RVMD) +9% - Shares advanced after the companies were named among the first nine recipients of the FDA Commissioner’s National Priority Review vouchers, designed to accelerate review timelines for nationally important medicines. Achieve Life Sciences’ Cytisinicline targets nicotine vaping addiction, Disc Medicine’s Bitopertin addresses porphyria, and Revolution Medicines’ RMC-6236 is for pancreatic cancer.
  • American Express (AXP) +5% - Shares jumped after raising the lower end of its 2025 earnings guidance, following Q3 results that topped Wall Street estimates. Strong cardholder spending and a successful Platinum Card relaunch drove network volume growth. The company now expects FY2025 EPS of $15.20–$15.50 (midpoint $15.35), up from $15.00–$15.50 previously, vs. the consensus of $15.34. It also raised its revenue growth outlook to 9%–10% from 8%–10%, implying 2025 revenue of $71.8B–$72.5B, slightly above the $71.6B analyst consensus.

Biggest stock losers

  • Novo Nordisk (NVO) -5% and Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) -4% - Shares of both drugmakers declined after U.S. President Donald Trump announced plans to significantly lower the price of weight-loss medications such as Ozempic. Speaking at a White House event on Thursday, Trump said the monthly cost of the Novo Nordisk-made drug could fall from about $1,000 to $150, sparking investor concerns about pricing pressure across the sector, including Lilly’s rival treatments Mounjaro and Zepbound.
  • Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) -4% - Shares slid following a report that the company plans to halt sales of server chips to data centers in China, following a prolonged business slump linked to Beijing’s 2023 ban on its products in critical infrastructure. The move underscores lingering tensions between the U.S. and China, as Micron was the first American chipmaker targeted by Beijing in what was widely viewed as retaliation for U.S. tech export curbs. According to Reuters, Micron will continue supplying chips to two Chinese customers with major data center operations outside China, including Lenovo (LNVGY), as well as to clients in the automotive and mobile sectors. Mainland China accounted for $3.4B, or 12%, of Micron’s total revenue in its last fiscal year.
  • CleanSpark (CLSK) -3% - Crypto-related stocks traded lower amid continued bitcoin liquidations and a broader market sell-off, Bloomberg reported. Around $80M worth of bitcoin positions were liquidated in the past 24 hours, according to CoinCentral data. BTC-USD was down 2.2% at $105,534 on Friday. Other notable decliners included MicroStrategy (MSTR), Circle Internet (CRCL), Bullish (BLSH), Riot Platforms (RIOT), MARA Holdings (MARA), Coinbase (COIN), Bit Digital (BTBT), Robinhood (HOOD), Hut 8 (HUT), HIVE Digital (HIVE), and IREN (IREN), all falling between 2% and 4%.
  • Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) -3% - U.S. bank stocks declined in premarket trading Friday, signaling a continuation of Thursday’s selloff sparked by mounting loan concerns at regional lenders. With trading volumes in smaller bank shares thinning, major Wall Street institutions led the decline: Wells Fargo (NYSE:WFC) fell 2%, JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) lost 1.3%, while Citigroup (NYSE:C) and Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS) each dropped 2%. The renewed pressure on the sector rippled through global markets, dragging down European financials and Asian equities as well.

data.title: U.S. buys Argentine pesos in Blue Chip Swap, spot markets, Bessent says
data.description:
The U.S. on Thursday purchased Argentine pesos (ARS:USD) in the "Blue Chip Swap" and spot markets, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said on social media platform X.

Last week, the U.S. Treasury finalized a $20B currency swap framework with Argentina and directly purchased Argentine pesos (ARS:USD).

"Treasury remains in close communication with Argentina's economic team as they work to Make Argentina Great Again. Treasury is monitoring all markets, and we have the capacity to act with flexibility and with force to stabilize Argentina," Bessent said in the post. 

The post comes a day after reports suggested that big U.S. banks were in discussions with the Treasury Department to provide up to $20B in emergency loans. 

After finalizing a $20B currency swap framework last week with Argentina, the U.S. has been looking to double the aid to $40B through a combination of financing from sovereign funds and the private sector.

Regarding the aid package, U.S. President Donald Trump had warned earlier that while he wanted to help, he would pull the deal if President Javier Milei does not win the October 26 midterm elections.

Argentina's economy has been battling challenges for the past few months. In April, the International Monetary Fund reached a preliminary agreement with Argentina on a $20 billion bailout.

The country's benchmark, the S&P MERVAL Index (MERVAL) was up 0.9%. The Global X MSCI Argentina ETF (NYSEARCA:ARGT), which provides U.S. investors with exposure to Argentine equities, rose ~0.1%.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: BNP Paribas sees tariff-driven inflation picking up, but cooling by 2026
data.description:
BNP Paribas expects U.S. inflation dynamics to shift in the coming quarters, with tariff effects set to play a more prominent role through the end of 2025 and into 2026.

The bank projects that while shelter costs are likely to ease, price pressures in non-shelter services should remain relatively firm, albeit at a slower pace than earlier this year. 

For the upcoming September Consumer Price Index (CPI) report, BNP Paribas believes the risks lean to the downside.

Softer goods prices and a moderation in housing-related inflation could offset the impact of seasonal strength in service categories, the investment institution said. Historically, core CPI readings in September have tended to come in slightly below market expectations, the analysts noted. 

The Bureau of Labor Statistics plans to release the September CPI data on October 24, though BNP Paribas warned that a prolonged federal government shutdown could delay the October report, currently slated for November 13. 

Looking ahead, the bank forecasts U.S. core CPI inflation to climb to about 3.8% annualized over the next six months, compared with roughly 3.0% year to date, before gradually cooling to near 2.5% in the second half of 2026, signaling a slow but steady disinflation trend.

Treasury ETFs: (NASDAQ:TLT), (NYSEARCA:TLH), (NASDAQ:IEF), (NASDAQ:IEI), (NASDAQ:SHY), (NYSEARCA:SGOV), (NYSEARCA:SCHO), and (NYSEARCA:BIL).

Bond ETFs: (NYSEARCA:AGG), (NASDAQ:BND), (NASDAQ:VCIT), (NYSEARCA:MUB), (NASDAQ:MBB), (NYSEARCA:JNK), (NYSEARCA:LQD), (NYSEARCA:HYG), and (NYSEARCA:TIP).

Inflation Protection ETFs: (NASDAQ:VTIP), (NYSEARCA:TIP), (NYSEARCA:SCHP), STIP), (NYSEARCA:TIPX), (NYSEARCA:SPIP), (NYSEARCA:WIP), (BATS:GTIP), (BATS:LQDI), and (NYSEARCA:RINF).

data.title: Eastern Bankshares gains on report activist takes stake, pushes for sale
data.description:
Eastern Bankshares (NASDAQ:EBC) rose 3% after a report that activist investor HoldCo Asset Management has taken a stake and is pushing for the company to pursue a sale.

HoldCo, which has built a 3% stake in Eastern Bankshares (NASDAQ:EBC) in recent months, is pressing for a sale, potentially to northeast super-regional M&T Bank (MTB), according to a  Reuters report on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter. It also wants Eastern management to stop its "poor allocation of capital."

HoldCo may raise pressure on Eastern Bankshares (EBC) and could start a proxy fight to oust directors, according to the report. Eastern Bankshares has a market cap of $3.4 billion.

Eastern Bankshares (EBC) didn't immediately respond to Reuters request for comment. 

Eastern Bankshares (EBC) is scheduled to report Q3 results on Thursday.
